title: Mixed Vegetable Pakora

overview.description: Crisp Indian vegetable fritters made with mixed vegetables, gram flour and aromatic spices.

ingredients: 150g potatoes, cut into very thin matchsticks or grated
½ tsp chilli powder, or to taste
method: Place the prepared vegetables in a large bowl.
Add the salt and mix thoroughly.
Leave for 15–20 minutes so the salt draws moisture from the vegetables.
Add the gram flour, cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, chilli powder, ajwain if using and baking powder.
Add the fresh coriander and green chilli.
Add the water a little at a time until the vegetables are lightly coated and the mixture holds together when pressed.
Take small loose handfuls of the mixture and carefully drop them into the hot oil.
Fry in batches, taking care not to overcrowd the pan.
Cook for approximately 4–6 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crisp.
Drain on a wire rack or kitchen paper.

learning.did-you-know: Pakora are found in many regional variations across South Asia, and the word generally refers to vegetables or other ingredients coated in a seasoned batter and fried.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a light fritter mixture using the moisture naturally released from vegetables rather than a large quantity of batter.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive and adaptable snack that uses common vegetables and works well as a family snack, starter or side dish.
chapman-tips: Keep the vegetables relatively thin so they cook through quickly.
Salting the vegetables first helps release moisture and reduces the amount of added water needed.
The mixture should be vegetable-heavy rather than a thick batter.
Don't make the pakoras too large or the outside may brown before the centre cooks.
Loose, irregular shapes usually produce the best crisp texture.
Ajwain adds a lovely savoury, slightly thyme-like flavour.
A wire rack helps prevent the pakoras from becoming soggy after frying.
For extra crispness, fry the pakoras in two stages: cook them through first, then fry briefly at a higher temperature.
notes.batch-cooking: Good for batch cooking. Freeze after frying and reheat in an oven or air fryer for the best texture.
notes.leftovers: Reheat in an oven or air fryer until hot and crisp.
